title: Ceraia equitans
taxonomy: Ceraia equitans is a species of moth in the family Sphingidae. This species was previously known as Acherontia cerastes, but it has been reclassified. Ceraia equitans belongs to the order Lepidoptera. The classification of Ceraia equitans is further divided into the kingdom Animalia and the phylum Arthropoda. The species is also known as the death's-head hawkmoth, a name that is often associated with the genus Acherontia, from which it was previously classified.

intro: Ceraia equitans, also known as Equisetum, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the Equisetaceae family and is one of the oldest living plant species on Earth. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin issues, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ceraia equitans include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as topical creams and ointments for skin conditions.
